content:
# Luke Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), PDF pages 87-88 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/LUK_003_page_001.jpg, LUK_003_page_002.jpg

---

**1.** In the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judea, and Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, and Philip his brother being tetrarch of the region of Iturea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias being tetrarch of Abilene,

**2.** upon [the] high-priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, [the] word of God came upon John the [son] of Zacharias in the wilderness.

**3.** And he came into all the surrounding-region of the Jordan, proclaiming [a] baptism of repentance unto forgiveness of sins,

**4.** as it has been written in [the] book of [the] words of Isaiah the prophet: "[A] voice of [one] crying-out in the wilderness, 'Prepare the way of [the] Lord, make straight his paths;

**5.** every ravine shall be filled-up, and every mountain and hill shall be made-low, and the crooked [things] shall become into straight [ways], and the rough [ways] into smooth roads;

**6.** and all flesh shall see the salvation of God.'"

**7.** He was saying therefore to the crowds going-out to be baptized by him, "Offspring of vipers, who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?

**8.** Produce therefore fruits worthy of repentance, and do not begin to say within yourselves, 'We have Abraham [as our] father.' For I say to you that God is able out of these stones to raise-up children to Abraham.

**9.** And already also the axe is laid to the root of the trees; every tree therefore not producing good fruit is cut-down and cast into fire."

**10.** And the crowds were questioning him, saying, "What then shall we do?"

**11.** And answering he was saying to them, "The [one] having two tunics, let him share with the [one] having not, and the [one] having foods, let him do likewise."

**12.** And tax-collectors also came to be baptized, and they said to him, "Teacher, what shall we do?"

**13.** And he said to them, "Collect nothing more beyond what has been appointed to you."

**14.** And [those] serving-as-soldiers also were questioning him, saying, "And what shall we ourselves do?" And he said to them, "Extort no one, neither accuse-falsely, and be content with your wages."

**15.** And the people being-in-expectation, and all [of them] deliberating in their hearts concerning John, whether perhaps he himself might be the Christ,

**16.** John answered, saying to all, "I indeed baptize you with water; but the [one] mightier than I is coming, of whom I am not worthy to loose the strap of his sandals; he himself shall baptize you in [the] Holy Spirit and fire;

**17.** whose winnowing-fork [is] in his hand, to thoroughly-cleanse his threshing-floor, and to gather the grain into his storehouse, but the chaff he will burn-up with unquenchable fire."

**18.** Indeed therefore, exhorting many [other things] also, he was proclaiming-good-news to the people.

**19.** But Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him concerning Herodias the wife of his brother, and concerning all the evil [things] which Herod did,

**20.** added also this upon all [things]: he shut-up John in prison.

**21.** And it came-to-pass, in the being-baptized of all the people, and Jesus having been baptized and praying, the heaven was opened,

**22.** and the Holy Spirit descended in bodily form as a dove upon him, and a voice came out of heaven, "You are my Son, the Beloved; in you I was well-pleased."

**23.** And Jesus himself was beginning [to be] about thirty years [old] when he began [his ministry], being — as was being supposed — [the] son of Joseph, [the son] of Heli,

**24.** [the son] of Matthat, [the son] of Levi, [the son] of Melchi, [the son] of Jannai, [the son] of Joseph,

**25.** [the son] of Mattathias, [the son] of Amos, [the son] of Nahum, [the son] of Esli, [the son] of Naggai,

**26.** [the son] of Maath, [the son] of Mattathias, [the son] of Semein, [the son] of Josech, [the son] of Joda,

**27.** [the son] of Joanan, [the son] of Rhesa, [the son] of Zerubbabel, [the son] of Shealtiel, [the son] of Neri,

**28.** [the son] of Melchi, [the son] of Addi, [the son] of Cosam, [the son] of Elmadam, [the son] of Er,

**29.** [the son] of Joshua, [the son] of Eliezer, [the son] of Jorim, [the son] of Matthat, [the son] of Levi,

**30.** [the son] of Symeon, [the son] of Judah, [the son] of Joseph, [the son] of Jonam, [the son] of Eliakim,

**31.** [the son] of Melea, [the son] of Menna, [the son] of Mattatha, [the son] of Nathan, [the son] of David,

**32.** [the son] of Jesse, [the son] of Obed, [the son] of Boaz, [the son] of Sala, [the son] of Nahshon,

**33.** [the son] of Amminadab, [the son] of Admin, [the son] of Arni, [the son] of Hezron, [the son] of Perez, [the son] of Judah,

**34.** [the son] of Jacob, [the son] of Isaac, [the son] of Abraham, [the son] of Terah, [the son] of Nahor,

**35.** [the son] of Serug, [the son] of Reu, [the son] of Peleg, [the son] of Eber, [the son] of Shelah,

**36.** [the son] of Cainan, [the son] of Arphaxad, [the son] of Shem, [the son] of Noah, [the son] of Lamech,

**37.** [the son] of Methuselah, [the son] of Enoch, [the son] of Jared, [the son] of Mahalaleel, [the son] of Cainan,

**38.** [the son] of Enosh, [the son] of Seth, [the son] of Adam, [the son] of God.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v. 2 — "upon" (epi):** Sinaiticus reads EPI for the preposition governing the high-priesthood, rendered literally "upon" rather than the smoother "during." The singular ARXIEREWS (high-priesthood) governs both Annas and Caiaphas jointly.

2. **v. 2 — "upon John" (epi Ioannen):** Sinaiticus uses EPI with the accusative for the word of God coming "upon" John, preserving the directional force of the preposition, whereas some later manuscripts read "to" (pros).

3. **v. 8 — "fruits" (karpous):** Sinaiticus reads the plural KARPOYC ("fruits"), where some manuscripts (including the Byzantine tradition) read the singular KARPON ("fruit").

4. **v. 19 — "wife of his brother":** Sinaiticus reads TH GYNAIKI TOY ADELFOY AYTOY ("the wife of his brother") without specifying Philip by name at this point; the brother's name Philip is supplied in some later manuscripts but is not explicit here in Sinaiticus.

5. **v. 22 — "You are my Son, the Beloved; in you I was well-pleased":** Sinaiticus reads SY EI O YIOC MOY O AGAPHTOC EN SOI EYDOKHSA, aligning with the standard form of the heavenly declaration. Some Western witnesses (notably Codex Bezae) read instead "You are my Son, today I have begotten you" (a quotation of Psalm 2:7), but Sinaiticus preserves the more widely attested reading.

6. **v. 23 — "being about thirty years":** Sinaiticus reads WSEI ETWN TRIAKONTA — "about of years thirty." The word order and construction are preserved literally, with WSEI ("about/as-if") indicating approximation.

7. **v. 33 — Genealogical variant (Admin/Arni):** Sinaiticus reads the genealogy through ADMIN and ARNI between Amminadab and Hezron. This differs from the later Byzantine text which typically reads "Aminadab, [the son] of Aram." The Sinaiticus reading preserves an additional generation in the genealogy, yielding 77 names total from Jesus to God — a number that may be theologically significant (7 x 11).

8. **v. 33 — "Perez, [the son] of Judah":** Sinaiticus includes both Perez and Judah in this verse, continuing the genealogical chain without a verse break, whereas some versification traditions place Judah at the start of v. 34.

9. **v. 36 — "Cainan":** Sinaiticus includes KAINAM (Cainan) between Arphaxad and Shelah, following the Septuagint tradition (Gen 10:24 LXX, 11:12-13 LXX). This name is absent from the Hebrew Masoretic Text and from some later Greek manuscripts.

10. **General — Itacisms and orthographic features:** Sinaiticus exhibits numerous itacistic spellings (e.g., EI for I, AI for E) characteristic of 4th-century scriptorial practice. These have been normalized in the translation but do not affect the meaning. The scribe (Scribe A for this portion) writes in continuous uncial script (scriptio continua) with no word separation.

11. **General — Nomina sacra:** Divine names and titles are abbreviated in Sinaiticus using the standard nomina sacra system: IC XC (Jesus Christ), KC (Lord), QC (God), PNA (Spirit), ANOC (human/man). These have been expanded in translation.
